narrative: OMX30 enters Thursday on the back of a powerful Asia-US risk rally, with NASDAQ's +2.02% and Nikkei's +5.58% overnight providing strong positive momentum. Locally, the index sits +10.3% above the 200-day moving average in confirmed bull regime, supported by a bullish MACD crossover and 90-day ROC of +10.7%. Oil's -3.22% retreat to $98 eases input-cost concerns for heavy industrials, while gold's +1.48% and copper's +1.26% directly support Boliden and mining equities. Technicals show RSI 53.29 (neutral, no mean reversion flag) and ADX 21 (moderate trend strength), placing us in the upper half of the Bollinger band at %B=0.74. The main risk is reversal from the 52W high proximity (-2.4%), and US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET could introduce intraday volatility.
morningBrief: Open with a bullish tilt on synchronized global rally carryover—Asia-US combo is hard to fight, and our MACD is in fresh bullish territory. Watch the 3180 level (50-MA); a break above triggers an acceleration run toward 3200+. Support anchors at 3085 (lower Bollinger band). Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo, Sandvik) should outperform today as oil weakness cuts headwinds and the weak USD/SEK backdrop (USD/SEK -0.52%, but still elevated) aids export competitiveness. Banks trade cautiously—US 10Y yield down 1.36% is a NIM headwind despite positive risk sentiment. Boliden is a tactical long: copper +1.26% and gold +1.48% hit the register for miners. Avoid fade rallies ahead of US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET; data could spike volatility. Close watch on Ericsson/Hexagon for tech sentiment; NASDAQ strength overnight is constructive but overbought (K=79.1) warns of intraday chop.
drivers: Strong Asia overnight (Nikkei +5.58%, Hang Seng +1.57%) with positive US carryover (S&P +1.46%, NASDAQ +2.02%)
OMX30 in confirmed bull market: +10.3% above 200-MA, MACD bullish crossover, ROC-90 at +10.7%
Commodity tailwind: gold +1.48% and copper +1.26% support mining/materials; oil pullback (-3.22%) eases input cost pressure on industrials
Technical setup remains constructive despite proximity to 52W high (-2.4%); RSI 53.29 neutral avoids mean reversion signal; ADX 21 supports trend strength

sectorImpacts.reason: Oil down 3.22% eases input cost pressure; weak USD/SEK backdrop (though recovering) supports export pricing; strong industrial sentiment from global rally carryover.
Gold +1.48% and copper +1.26% are direct revenue drivers for Boliden; SSAB benefits from industrial activity and moderating oil/energy costs.
US 10Y yield down 1.36% to 4.356% compresses NIM expectations; positive risk rally sentiment is offset by falling rate tailwind.
NASDAQ +2.02% overnight lifts sentiment; however, Stochastic K=79.1 warns of near-term overbought conditions—expect intraday profit-taking.
US 10Y yield down 1.36% reduces near-term refinancing pressure, but longer-term growth concerns from falling yields present overhang.
Oil weakness is marginally supportive for consumer spending; EUR/SEK down 0.08% adds modest sourcing cost headwind for H&M. Risk rally favors discretionary but sentiment remains cautious.

watchList.reason: Gold +1.48% and copper +1.26% provide direct upside catalysts; positioned at ideal risk/reward on commodity strength.
Oil -3.22% reduces customer input-cost pressure; global industrial momentum (Nikkei +5.58%, S&P +1.46%) supports order flow; export-sensitive upside.
NASDAQ +2.02% overnight is constructive, but Stochastic K=79.1 flags overbought conditions; expect intraday profit-taking.
watchList.catalyst: Tech sentiment vs. mean reversion risk

watchList.reason: US 10Y -1.36% is a NIM headwind offsetting positive risk rally sentiment; better entry on relative weakness.
EUR/SEK weakness (-0.08%) adds sourcing cost pressure; oil down slightly supports discretionary but sentiment remains muted.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases today. US Jobless Claims (initial jobless claims) at 14:30 CET is the key macro event; volatility spike likely if claims miss forecasts.
topRisk: US Jobless Claims surprise at 14:30 CET could trigger sharp intraday reversal; proximity to 52W high (-2.4%) leaves OMX30 vulnerable to profit-taking if risk sentiment falters. Geopolitical escalation (Iran-US tensions) could spike VIX and unwind commodity gains.
technicalSummary: MACD bullish crossover active; histogram +0.989 (strong momentum)
RSI 53.29 neutral (no mean reversion flag); Stochastic K=79.1 overbought intraday
ADX 21 moderate trend strength with bullish DI bias
Bollinger %B=0.74 (upper half), width 5.26% suggests room to run
At 90% of 52W range, -2.4% from high: reversal risk present
